case_title: KARLA SCHLAGS VS. CLOUD 9 LIMOSUINE, INC et al

OBJECTION TO HEARING BEFORE A DISCOVERY COMMISSIONER AS TEMPORARY JUDGE FILED BY DEFENDANT CLOUD 9 LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CLOUD 9 LIMOSUINE, INC.)
REPLY FOR DEFT'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CLOUD 9 LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CLOUD 9 LIMOSUINE, INC.)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CLOUD 9 LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON CARRIER LIABILITY AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS CONCEDE THAT COMMON CARRIER LIABILITY DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS CASE. AT PAGE 5 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT PROVIDED ALCOHOL AND/OR DRUGS TO THE INTOXICATED MINORS. THERE IS A FACTUAL ISSUE NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON DEMURRER WHETHER THE EXCEPTION TO IMMUNITY UNDER BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE 25602.1 APPLIES TO THIS CASE. DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY NOT TO PROVIDE DRUGS TO INTOXICATED MINORS. PLAINTIFFS ARE REQUIRED TO FILE THEIR OPPOSITION IN THIS CASE. PLAINTIFFS MISTAKENLY FILED THE OPPOSITION IN CASE NUMBER 521553. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL HALE, ESQ., LIUZZI, MURPHY, SOLOMON & AIKINS, 415.400.7002, FOR PLAINTIFF KARLA SCHLAGS.
KEVIN ALLEN, ESQ., LOW BALL & LYNCH, 415.981.6630, FOR DEFENDANT CLOUD 9 LIMOUSINE, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CLOUD 9 LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for common carrier liability and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs concede that common carrier liability does not apply to this case. At page 5 of the amended complaint plaintiffs allege that defendant provided alcohol and/or drugs to the intoxicated minors. There is a factual issue not amenable to resolution on demurrer whether the exception to immunity under Business and Professions Code 25602.1 applies to this case. Defendant owed a duty not to provide drugs to intoxicated minors. Plaintiffs are required to file their opposition in this case. Plaintiffs mistakenly filed the opposition in case number 521553. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
